summary: Matched and linked src/melee/gm/gmmultiman.c by replacing several nonmatching stubs with C implementations, promoting the object from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, and making supporting type/prototype corrections in gm_16F1, gmmain_lib, and gm/types.h. The work also localized previously extern gmmultiman BSS/data symbols as static definitions, added required includes for ground and time helpers, adjusted minor-scene constants to named menu enums, and used a forced-active padding word to preserve BSS layout.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/gm/gmmultiman.c is built as Object(Matching, ...) instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).
Implemented several previously stubbed gmmultiman functions, including gm_801B632C, gm_801B63C4, gm_801B6428, and gm_801B65D4.

Converted gmmultiman file-local storage from extern declarations to static definitions for gm_80490960, gm_80490AA8, gm_80490BE0, gm_804950F0, gm_80495238, gm_80495370, gm_804D68E8, gm_804D68E9, gm_804D68F0, and gm_804D68F1.
Added #include <melee/gr/ground.h> and #include <melee/lb/lbtime.h> to support newly matched logic that calls Ground_801C1DE4 and lbTime_8000AF74.
Inserted a static u32 pad guarded by #pragma force_active on between gm_804D68E9 and gm_804D68F0, indicating a deliberate layout/linking preservation tactic for active BSS/data ordering.

Updated MinorScene table literals from hex values such as 0x08 and 0x02 to named constants MN_CSS and MN_VS where visible in the excerpt, while also normalizing small numeric literals like 0x00/0x01/0x03 to decimal 0/1/3.
Moved gm_801B6AD8_inline earlier in gmmultiman.c and changed it from static inline to static void, likely to satisfy ordering or inlining/codegen constraints.
Corrected gm_801733D8 return type from int to u8 and gm_8017341C return type from int to u16 in both gm_16F1.c and gm_16F1.h.
Changed gmMainLib_8015D438 return type from s32* to u32* in gmmain_lib.c and gmmain_lib.h.
Changed struct gmm_x1F2C field x94 from s32 to u32 in gm/types.h, matching its use as an unsigned frame/time or bit-compatible value.
Changed struct gmm_x0_584_t fields unk_585 and unk_586 from s8 to u8 in gm/types.h, aligning with gmmultiman usage as unsigned color/parameter bytes.
Changed a local pointer in gm_16AE.c, temp_r30_2, from s32* to u32* to align with the corrected gmMainLib_8015D438 signature.
smart_moves: Linked the matching unit only after cleaning up supporting type signatures, avoiding a narrow local match that would leave incorrect headers or mismatched callers elsewhere.

Used unsigned narrow return types for gm_801733D8 and gm_8017341C where the surrounding code compares against character/stage-like constants and passes values into functions expecting u8/u16-sized data.

Propagated the u32 interpretation of gmm_x1F2C.x94 through the struct field, gmMainLib_8015D438 API, and a dependent caller in gm_16AE.c, keeping type fixes consistent across declaration, definition, and use.
Defined gmmultiman-owned BSS/data symbols statically in gmmultiman.c instead of leaving them as externs, which is important for actually linking the translation unit as matching.
Preserved an apparent layout gap with #pragma force_active on and a static pad, a targeted tactic for keeping symbol placement without relying on unused-variable elimination behavior.
Replaced magic menu IDs in MinorScene entries with MN_CSS and MN_VS where applicable, improving readability without changing the scene table structure.
Kept the match-oriented C close to observed game logic: setup functions populate CSSData/StartMeleeData, preload cache state, match exit handling, record bookkeeping, and pending scene transitions.
decomp_lessons: When moving a file from NonMatching to Matching, verify local static data ownership and BSS ordering; extern placeholders may need to become file-local definitions to link correctly.

Small signedness mistakes in shared structs and headers can block matching in unrelated game-mode code; propagate type corrections through the field, accessor, header prototype, implementation, and all callers.
For game-mode scene files, MinorScene table matching may depend not only on callbacks but also exact data-object placement and padding around static variables.
Unsigned byte fields are often more appropriate for character IDs, colors, and packed mode parameters than s8, especially when values are passed to APIs using u8 or compared with enum-like constants.
Frame counts and record values that are manipulated with bit masks or monotonic comparisons may need u32 storage and pointer types even if earlier guesses used s32.
Named constants such as MN_CSS and MN_VS can be used in matched data tables when they compile to the same values, improving maintainability while preserving binary output.
Moving or de-inlining helpers can be a legitimate matching tactic; helper placement and inline qualification affect generated code and should be tuned based on evidence.
If a matched function needs a previously undeclared library call, add the precise header include instead of relying on implicit declarations or extern guesses.
naming_conventions: Preserved address-based function names such as gm_801B632C, gm_801B63C4, gm_801B6428, gm_801B65D4, gm_801B67E8_OnInit, and gm_801B6AD8_inline.
Used subsystem prefixes consistently: gm_ for game-mode functions/data, gmMainLib_ for main game-mode library helpers, lbDvd_ and lbTime_ for library calls, and Ground_ for ground subsystem access.
Kept global/static data symbols named by address, such as gm_804D68E8 and gm_803DECB8_MinorScenes, matching existing decomp project style.
Retained inferred struct field names such as unk_584, unk_585, unk_586, x94, x1F2C, and unk_530 where semantic names were not yet established.
Used a helper suffix '_inline' for gm_801B6AD8_inline even after changing it to static void, indicating the name came from a previous or intended inlining/matching role.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promoted gmmultiman.c to Matching in configure.py after adding C implementations and resolving dependent type mismatches.
Used #pragma push / #pragma force_active on / #pragma pop around a static u32 pad to force retention of padding needed for symbol/layout matching.
Converted signed pointer and field types to unsigned forms, including s32* to u32* and s32 to u32, to influence loads, stores, comparisons, or register use in matched code.
Changed narrow return types from int to u8/u16 in gm_16F1 to better match call-site codegen and ABI expectations for character/stage-like return values.
Adjusted helper declaration from static inline to static void and relocated it before gm_801B69C0, suggesting codegen/order sensitivity was part of the match.
Introduced PAD_STACK(4) in gm_801B65D4, indicating explicit stack-shape control was needed for the matched function.
Used a local block around PreloadCacheScene* scene in gm_801B6428, likely to shape variable lifetime/register allocation for matching.
Preserved a goto label block_22 in gm_801B65D4 rather than over-structuring the control flow, a common tactic when matching branch layout.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but no human text excerpt was provided, so its content cannot be summarized.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type struct gmm_x0_584_t fields unk_584, unk_585, and unk_586.
Search for additional users of gmMainLib_8015D438 to confirm all callers agree with the u32* return type.
Inspect later gmmultiman.c work for semantic names replacing gm_801B632C, gm_801B6428, gm_801B65D4, and gm_801B6AD8_inline.
Look for other PRs using #pragma force_active padding around BSS/data symbols as a layout-preservation tactic.
Check whether gm_80490BE0 being declared as MatchExitInfo[2] for padding is refined or documented in later headers.
